zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Margaret Abbes於1520年12月3日出生在英國素福爾可納易藍德時，她的父親George Abbes25歲，母親Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn23歲。她於1540年與John Mawle Jr.在英國素福爾可納易藍德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1586年12月3日在她的家鄉享年66歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可納易藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Margaret Abbes于1520年12月3日出生在英国素福爾可納易藍德时，她的父亲George Abbes25岁，母亲Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn23岁。她于1540年与John Mawle Jr.在英国素福爾可納易藍德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1586年12月3日在她的家乡享年66岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可納易藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Abbes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Syed, Ghulam, Ali, Mohammad, Mohammed, Mohamed, Farhat, Abbas, Asad, Azhar, Ahmad, Amir.
Muslim: from the Arabic personal name ʿAbbās, literally ‘stern, austere’. Al-ʿAbbas ibn ʿAbd al-Muttalib was the name of an uncle of the prophet Muhammad (see Abbasi ).
Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Abbe, a pet form of Albert or Albrecht .

variants.name: Abbasi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammad, Ali, Abdul, Adnan, Tariq, Arshad, Mahmood, Mohammed, Mousa, Muhammad, Nadeem, Sami.

Muslim (Indian subcontinent and Iran): surname denoting descent from or association with someone called   Abbas  , in particular association with Al-‘Abbas ibn ‘Abd al-Muttalib, an uncle (died 652) of the prophet Muhammad, the ancestor of the Abbasid dynasty of caliphs, who ruled the Islamic world from 750 to 1258.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Margaret Abbes was born on 3 December 1520, in Nayland, Suffolk, England, her father, George Abbes, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn, was 23. She married John Mawle Jr. in 1540, in Nayland, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 3 December 1586, in her hometown, at the age of 66, and was buried in Nayland, Suffolk, England.

Elle a épousé John Mawle Jr. en 1540, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Margaret Abbes родилась 3 декабря 1520 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени George Abbes было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Mawle Jr. в 1540 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 3 декабря 1586 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия.
